plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to boost income and broad market exposure.
Diversify by adding XLV, a high-quality healthcare ETF.
This strategy prioritizes capital preservation and stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a stable ~1.5% monthly return with significantly reduced drawdown risk. It focuses on capital preservation through broad index and income-generating assets, minimizing leverage and concentration. Expect lower volatility but also slower growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and rebalance the portfolio.
Trim QQQI as flagged by scanner and reduce overall exposure.
Sell SOXL to remove a significant underperformer and reduce leverage.
Reduce SPAL to minimize leveraged ETF risk.
Increase CRWD exposure given its positive performance and add GOOGL for quality tech diversification.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% monthly growth by managing risk and reducing concentration. It balances broad index exposure with quality tech and minimal leverage. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and free up capital for high-conviction ideas.
Trim QQQI as flagged by the scanner, reallocating to growth.
Sell SOXL to exit a significant underperformer, even in an aggressive mode.
Increase SMH exposure, leveraging its strong scanner read and sector trend.
Initiate positions in CRDO and ANET, both high-scoring scanner setups with good risk/reward.
This strategy aims for maximum growth by concentrating in strong setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into high-conviction tech and semiconductor plays. It utilizes higher concentration in top-scoring scanner setups. Be aware that this approach significantly amplifies both potential upside and downside, with a higher risk of substantial drawdowns, and leveraged ETFs can experience decay.

plan: Trim SPY and QQQ to reduce broad market growth exposure.
Significantly increase QQQI to maximize income generation.
Sell SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta growth assets.
Eliminate all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to minimize risk.
This strategy prioritizes steady monthly distributions over capital appreciation.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions by rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad market exposure. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth. Remember that distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in a market selloff; this is not a savings account.
